label: Refinance & Equity Access
title: Refinance & Home Equity Access in BC
metaDescription: Refinance your mortgage or unlock home equity in Greater Vancouver. HELOCs, equity-based mortgages, purchase-plus-improvements and bridge financing — full strategy review.
intro: Your home is likely your most powerful financial tool. Used carelessly, it's expensive. Used strategically, it can pay down debt, fund renovations, finance an investment property or bridge a purchase.
body:
Greater Vancouver homeowners hold a remarkable amount of equity — the median detached home value across the Lower Mainland sits well over $1.5M, and most owners have lived through a decade of price appreciation. Refinancing or accessing that equity isn't always the right call, but when it is, the savings are real.

The five pages below cover the full toolkit: a straight rate-and-term refinance to lower your payment, a HELOC for revolving access, a purchase-plus-improvements mortgage for renovations rolled into the purchase, an equity-based mortgage for income-light files, and bridge financing for the awkward week between selling and buying.

highlights.title: Up to 80% LTV on refinance
highlights.description: Borrow against up to 80% of your home's value on a refinance — the federal cap.

highlights.title: Up to 65% LTV on HELOC alone
highlights.description: Standalone HELOCs are capped at 65% of value; combined mortgage + HELOC products can go to 80%.

highlights.title: Real cost analysis
highlights.description: I'll model the prepayment penalty, legal fees and break-even point so you know whether the move pays off.

faqs.question: How much can I refinance?
faqs.answer: Federally regulated lenders allow refinancing up to 80% loan-to-value (LTV). On a $1.5M home with $400k owing, you could in principle refinance up to $1.2M — pulling out $800k of equity, subject to qualifying on the new payment.

faqs.question: Refinance or HELOC?
faqs.answer: Refinance if you want a one-time chunk at a fixed lower rate. HELOC if you want flexible, revolving access at a variable rate. Many clients use both: refinance the mortgage and add a HELOC behind it.

faqs.question: How long does a refinance take?
faqs.answer: Two to four weeks from application to funding, assuming an appraisal slot is available and your documents are clean. Discharge from the old lender adds another few business days.
